fields.summary: Specifies CICP (Coding-independent Code Points) color primaries as defined in ITU-T H.273.
fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKColorspacePrimariesCicp` enumerates the color primaries defined by the CICP system
(ITU-T H.273 / ISO 23091-2). These values are used as the `colorPrimaries` parameter
for <xref:SkiaSharp.SKColorSpace.CreateCicp(SkiaSharp.SKColorspacePrimariesCicp,SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp)>
to construct a color space from standard identifiers rather than raw primaries matrices.

Each member corresponds to a numeric CICP code point. Use `Unknown` when the primaries
are not specified or not among the recognized values.

## Examples

```csharp
// Create an sRGB-equivalent color space using CICP
var colorSpace = SKColorSpace.CreateCicp(
    SKColorspacePrimariesCicp.Rec709,
    SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp.Iec6196621);
```
]]></format>

fields.summary: Primaries defined by ITU-T H.273 for code point 22.
ITU-R BT.2020 / BT.2100 primaries (CICP code point 9), used for wide color gamut HDR content.
ITU-R BT.470 System B/G primaries (CICP code point 5), used for European standard-definition television.
ITU-R BT.470 System M primaries (CICP code point 4), used for NTSC standard-definition television.
ITU-R BT.601 primaries (CICP code point 6), used for standard-definition digital video.
ITU-R BT.709 primaries (CICP code point 1), used for HDTV and sRGB content.
SMPTE EG 432-1 (P3-D65) primaries (CICP code point 12), used for digital cinema and wide color gamut displays.
SMPTE RP 431-2 (DCI-P3) primaries (CICP code point 11), used for digital cinema projection.
SMPTE ST 240 primaries (CICP code point 7), used for early HDTV standards.
SMPTE ST 428-1 (XYZ) primaries (CICP code point 10), used for digital cinema distribution.
Unknown or unspecified primaries (CICP code point 0).
